jira.description: Jira issue tracking — search, create, update, comment, transition issues

omega-memory.description: Persistent agent memory with semantic search, multi-agent coordination, and knowledge graphs — run via uvx (richer than the basic memory store)

exa-web-search.description: Web search, research, and data ingestion via Exa API — prefer task-scoped use for broader research after GitHub search and primary docs

context7.description: Live documentation lookup — use with /docs command and documentation-lookup skill (resolve-library-id, query-docs).

filesystem.description: Filesystem operations — set the path to your projects root, e.g. /home/username/projects or $HOME/projects

playwright.description: Browser automation and testing via Playwright

devfleet.description: Multi-agent orchestration — dispatch parallel Claude Code agents in isolated worktrees. Plan projects, auto-chain missions, read structured reports. Repo: https://github.com/LEC-AI/claude-devfleet

token-optimizer.description: Token optimization for 95%+ context reduction via content deduplication and compression

_comments.usage: Copy the servers you need to your ~/.claude.json mcpServers section
_comments.env_vars: Replace YOUR_*_HERE placeholders with actual values
_comments.disabling: Use ECC_DISABLED_MCPS=github,context7,... to disable bundled ECC MCPs during install/sync, or use disabledMcpServers in project config for per-project overrides
_comments.context_warning: Keep under 10 MCPs enabled to preserve context window

_recommended_start.description: Enable these 3 first — they cover the most common workflows

_optional.devfleet: Multi-agent parallelism — requires running the DevFleet server locally (see repo link in description)
_optional.exa-web-search: Deep web research — useful when GitHub search + Context7 docs are insufficient
_optional.omega-memory: Richer memory with semantic search — use instead of basic 'memory' server
_optional.jira: Issue tracking — useful if your team uses Jira
_optional.token-optimizer: Reduces context token usage — helpful for very large codebases
_optional.filesystem: Direct filesystem access — set path to your projects root before enabling

_setup_guide.github: Create a PAT at https://github.com/settings/tokens — required scopes: repo, read:org
_setup_guide.jira: API token from https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens — use your Jira login email
_setup_guide.exa-web-search: API key from https://exa.ai — sign up for a free or paid plan
